message:
feat(fnp-python): native recfunctions_unstructured_to_structured (br-franken_numpy-hswh)

Convert an N-D numeric array's trailing axis into named fields of a
caller-supplied structured dtype — the canonical "stack of records"
transform.

Algorithm:
1. Require an explicit structured dtype (names=None, align=false,
   casting='unsafe' — the defaults). Anything else delegates.
2. Pull dtype.names and assert one field per trailing-axis entry;
   compound / variable-width dtypes fall back.
3. Allocate np.zeros(source.shape[:-1], dtype=new_dtype) to carry the
   output records.
4. For each field i with name f, assign `out[f] = source[..., i]` by
   constructing the `(slice(None),) * (ndim-1) + (i,)` index tuple
   explicitly. Numpy's broadcasting writes the column into every
   record's f slot.

That mirrors recfunctions' own loop body without depending on the
recfunctions module.

Parity test's 3x3 float → (x,y,z) f4 structured array branch still
passes. Full suite: 395/395 lib tests green on rch.

hswh structured-helpers native coverage: rename_fields, drop_fields,
unstructured_to_structured. Still delegated: append_fields (fill_value
path), merge_arrays (side-by-side field stacking with padding).
